question_structure.context: As shown in the figure, a uniform electric field is formed between two parallel metal plates $A$ and $B$ carrying opposite charges. An electron is injected into the electric field along the central line perpendicular to the direction of the electric field with an initial velocity $v_{0} = 4 \times 10^{6} \, \mathrm{m/s}$ from point $O$. When the electron exits the electric field at point $C$ on the right edge, its velocity direction forms a $30^{\circ}$ angle with the direction of $v_{0}$. The charge of the electron is $e = 1.6 \times 10^{-19} \, \mathrm{C}$, and the mass of the electron is $m = 0.91 \times 10^{-30} \, \mathrm{kg}$.
question_structure.sub_question_1: What is the kinetic energy of the electron at point $C$ in $\mathrm{J}$?
question_structure.sub_question_2: What is the magnitude of the potential difference between points $O$ and $C$ in $\mathrm{V}$?

sub_question_1.step_1: According to the geometric triangle solution, the velocity of the electron at point $C$ is: $v_{t} = \frac{v_{0}}{\cos{30^{\circ}}}$
sub_question_1.step_3: By combining step_1 and step_2, we obtain: $E_{k} = \frac{1}{2}m\left(\frac{v_{0}}{\cos{30^{\circ}}}\right)^{2} = 9.7 \times 10^{-18}  \mathrm{J}$

sub_question_2.step_4: For an electron moving from $O$ to $C$, according to the work-energy theorem, we have: $e U = \frac{1}{2}mv_{t}^{2} - \frac{1}{2}mv_{0}^{2}$
sub_question_2.step_5: By combining step_1 and step_4, we obtain: $U = \frac{m(v_{t}^{2} - v_{0}^{2})}{2e} = 15.2  \mathrm{V}$

image_captions: A diagram depicts a charged particle of mass "m" and charge "e" moving with initial velocity "v0" horizontally between two oppositely charged parallel plates. The particle is deflected by the electric field downwards, emerging at an angle of 30 degrees relative to the initial trajectory. The top plate has a negative charge, and the bottom plate has a positive charge. The particle’s trajectory is shown using a dashed line.

result_quantity.name: Velocity of the electron at point C
Kinetic energy of the electron at point C
Potential energy change from O to C
Potential difference between points O and C
